candidates.problem_statement: Live attestation sends Bearer auth from sandbox auth file and currently receives HTTP 401.
rollback_expectations: capture byte-for-byte snapshot of sandbox auth.json before mutation
restore previous auth.json if live attestation remains blocked
if backend auth_ref rebinding is attempted, restore backend-registry.json atomically on failure
candidates.admissibility_note: This is the narrowest primary repair lane because live attestation currently fails before any managed active-lane proof can be earned.

candidates.problem_statement: Managed active lane is not materialized: sync keeps sandbox on stable, 8320 is absent, generated config/approved target are absent.
rollback_expectations: restore launcher/materialization artifacts to their prior states if live activation proof fails
remove generated/scratch materialization files on rollback
do not claim effective managed truth without post-launch live attestation
candidates.admissibility_note: This remains a later repair lane because materialization alone cannot truthfully clear the current HTTP 401 blocker.

candidates.problem_statement: A fused auth+materialization repair would mix secret/source mutation with launcher/materialization mutation in one pass.
rollback_expectations: would require dual rollback domains: auth source and materialization artifacts
would need to prove no partial cross-domain success is misreported
candidates.admissibility_note: Rejected as the immediate next contour because canon does not provide a fused owner primitive here and risk shapes are different.
